message:
style(e2e): clippy cleanup pass on benchmark/honesty/persistent phase + visibility tweak

Final clippy backlog clear on the e2e crate after the overlay
honesty gate / benchmark metadata commits earlier in this batch.
All changes are lint-driven; no behavior change.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/benchmark.rs

- metric_direction(): tag with #[allow(clippy::match_same_arms)].
  The match has three arms (HigherIsBetter / LowerIsBetter /
  Invariant) that share string-table lookup but compute their
  "improved" / "regressed" labels differently.  Two of the arms
  do happen to format the same string when changed==false, which
  is what trips the lint, but folding them obscures the per-arm
  intent and the future-proofing for adding new comparison
  modes.  The allow stays scoped to this function only.
- metric_improvement_pct(): merge HigherIsBetter and Invariant
  arms with `|` since they compute the identical
  ((candidate - baseline) / baseline) * 100.0 expression.
  LowerIsBetter still inverts the sign and stays in its own
  arm.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/benchmark_metadata.rs

- validate_benchmark_lifecycle_parity: visibility upgraded from
  `pub(crate)` to `pub` so the Phase 9 verification gate inside
  fsqlite-harness can call it directly without going through a
  re-export shim.  The function takes a workspace_root: &Path
  and returns Vec<String> violations, so it's already a clean
  public API shape.
- validate_benchmark_metadata_records: rewrite the
  `Some(Mode::Parity) => { if cond { push } }` and
  `Some(Mode::Control) => { if cond { push } }` arms as guarded
  match arms `Some(Mode::Parity) if cond => { push }` and
  `Some(Mode::Control) if cond => { push }`.  Same control flow,
  satisfies clippy::collapsible_match and reads as the
  conditional dispatch it actually is.  The empty None arm and
  the leftover Some-with-no-violations cases all collapse into a
  single `_ => {}`.
- parse_benchmark_meta_records: switch
  `for meta in pending_meta.drain(..)` to
  `for meta in std::mem::take(&mut pending_meta)`.  drain() over
  the full range allocates a Drain iterator and then frees the
  Vec's capacity at the end of the loop, while std::mem::take
  swaps in an empty Vec (zero allocation) and lets the moved Vec
  drop at the end of the loop.  Same end state, fewer
  allocations, and clippy::iter_with_drain doesn't fire.
- normalize_bench_dimension: replace
  `allowed.iter().any(|allowed_value| *allowed_value == value)`
  with the equivalent `allowed.contains(&value)`.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/overlay_honesty_gate.rs

- File-level
  `#![allow(clippy::derive_partial_eq_without_eq,
            clippy::derivable_impls,
            clippy::zero_sized_map_values)]`
  with an inline comment "Pedantic-only style preferences inside
  this module's gate-policy types".  The honesty gate types are
  intentionally PartialEq-but-not-Eq (Eq would foreclose adding
  f64 fields later), explicitly impl Default rather than deriving
  it (so the default tier can be documented inline), and use
  BTreeMap<K, ()> as a sorted set (zero-sized values are the
  point).  All three lints are correct in general but wrong for
  this module specifically.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/persistent_phase_audit.rs

- build_persistent_phase_component_comparison_report: tag with
  #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)].  The function takes the
  benchmark group name, concurrency, three pairs of
  (sqlite_baseline, frankensqlite_subject) phase data, and the
  output sink — collapsing those into an Args struct would
  force every call site through an extra builder for no real
  win.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/correctness_transactions.rs

- txn_concurrent_schema_and_dml_preserve_index_and_rows: hoist
  the `payload` and `category` strings into local lets before
  the format! call so the format! template can interpolate them
  by name.  Replaces the nested `format!("..{}..{}..", ..,
  format!("..{}", ..))` with `let payload = format!(...); let
  category = ...; let sql = format!("..{category}..{payload}..");`
  which satisfies clippy::format_in_format_args.  Both forms
  produce identical SQL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,3 +1,10 @@
+#![allow(
+    clippy::derive_partial_eq_without_eq,
+    clippy::derivable_impls,
+    clippy::zero_sized_map_values
+)]
+// Pedantic-only style preferences inside this module's gate-policy types.
+
 use std::collections::BTreeMap;
 use std::fmt;
 use std::path::Path;

@@ -160,6 +160,7 @@ fn component_row(
 }
 
 #[must_use]
+#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
 pub fn build_persistent_phase_component_comparison_report(
     benchmark_group: &str,
     concurrency: usize,
